title: salix rotundifolia
intro: Salix rotundifolia, also known as Round-leaved Willow or White Willow, is a type of deciduous shrub or small tree belonging to the Salicaceae family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, due to the presence of salicylic acid, which is similar to aspirin. Salix rotundifolia has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and alleviating inflammation, making it a popular remedy for conditions such as arthritis and menstrual cramps. Herbal preparations of Salix rotundifolia can be made into teas, infusions, or salves, and are often used to soothe skin irritations and wounds, as well as to treat respiratory issues like bronchitis and coughs.
